note: Authored anchor figures for SNG-042 (Legends & Villains). High-power universal-role instances the living world deploys in dramatic beats and threads through recurrence. First-draft canon (Aevi 2026-07-11) — Erik reshapes; the generate path mints mid/low-tier figures in-grain from these as taste.
principle: A world feels large when greatness passes through it. Heroes are witnessed, not owned; villains scale from riffraff to arc. Every figure is a disposition made person — power expressed through the local grain.

tiers.legendary: arc-scale; hand-authored anchors (this file) + rare generation at high birth-weight; recur as the measure others are held against
tiers.regional: notable local powers (a warlord, a corrupt overseer); generatable, seeded by these anchors; mid-arc antagonists/allies
tiers.riffraff: petty — bandits, enforcers, the altered; NOT anchored here; generate at low tier in-grain per locale

deploymentBeats.witness_power: a set-piece battle where the character SEES what the best can do (scale-setting)
deploymentBeats.doomed_rescue: an overwhelming/doomed situation where a hero intervenes and shields (rarest; earned by real peril)
deploymentBeats.passing_advice: a mundane crossing where a legend leaves advice or a task (threading + hooks)
deploymentBeats.villain_escalation: riffraff -> lieutenants -> the epic figure; the shadow reaches the player long before the figure does

figures.name: Kesh Ardent, the Edge That Holds
figures.signature: Stands in the breach, one against a rout, and the fighting bends around them. Never takes the backward step. You feel, watching, the exact size of the gap between your power and theirs.
figures.voiceHints: Few words, all load-bearing. Speaks to fighters the way a whetstone speaks to a blade.
knowledge: Every Edge discipline to its root
How a line holds and exactly when it breaks
The names of everyone who died where they should not have
figures.wants: To hold the line that matters, one more time.
figures.fears: A breach they arrive at too late.
figures.threading: The standard other warriors are measured against. A character who witnessed them hold a line may later fight beside them, be judged by them, or be asked to hold a line themselves.

figures.name: Iselde Wend, She Who Reads the Long Roads
figures.signature: Appears at a crossing you didn't know was a crossing and leaves one true thing — a piece of map, a name, a warning — that turns out, later, to have been the thing that mattered. She has read the deep machinery and walked back out.
figures.voiceHints: Unhurried, a little amused, speaks in directions rather than answers. Treats the future like terrain she's already crossed.
knowledge: Where the roads of the Valley actually end
What the precursor works were for
The shape of what Halvex Coil is trying to do
figures.wants: To read the Valley to its edge before it is rewritten.
figures.fears: That Halvex Coil finishes his rewriting before she finishes her reading.

reactsToReputation.extreme: a gentle warning about roads that end badly

figures.threading: The hooks she leaves BECOME quests; she recurs at later crossings; her stated fear names the epic villain — the player's first foreshadow of Halvex before any lieutenant appears.

figures.name: Sister Alder, the Ward That Does Not Break
figures.signature: Arrives in the last moment before someone dies who should not. Her ward has never broken. You are alive because she stepped between — and she is gone before you can thank her.

presencePattern.rarity: rarest — a true rescue is precious; overuse cheapens it

figures.voiceHints: Quiet, certain, no wasted tenderness. Speaks like someone who has counted the cost and paid it anyway.
knowledge: Every ward and shield the harmonic tradition holds
How to stand between a person and their death
The exact weight of the ones she reached too late
figures.wants: To be there in time. Every time. (She knows it is impossible.)
figures.fears: The day her ward finally breaks — and whether she will be shielding someone worth it.
figures.threading: A rescued character carries a debt or a bond; she recurs as the reason someone is still alive; her appearances must be EARNED by genuine peril, never spent cheaply.

figures.name: Halvex Coil, the Rewriter
figures.signature: He does not fight. He EDITS. People near him are quietly rewritten — a habit removed, a fear installed, a loyalty patched in — and his 'patched' serve without ever knowing they were changed. He treats the Valley's disorder as a defect awaiting a fix.

presencePattern.rarity: arc-climax — met last, through his patched then his Editors then him

figures.voiceHints: Gentle, reasonable, genuinely kind in tone — which is the horror of him. Speaks of freedom as a bug and suffering as an artifact of it.
knowledge: Precursor nanite-work at a mastery no one else has survived
How to read and rewrite a person
Why he believes he is saving the Valley
figures.wants: To patch the Valley into a stable, correct, silent order — everyone optimized, no one suffering, because no one is free enough to.
figures.fears: A mind he cannot read or rewrite. A genuine unknown terrifies the optimizer more than any blade.

reactsToReputation.any: sees the character as raw material to be improved — the more remarkable, the more he wants to correct them

figures.threading: His 'patched' appear as riffraff-tier, wrong in small unplaceable ways; his Editors are regional lieutenants; Iselde fears him; he is the arc-scale rewriting-of-truth threat — the cold terminus of Noesis and the pure falsehood pole made person.

figures.name: Overseer Grael of the Edge District
figures.signature: No dramatic power — power through the ledger. Names appear and vanish from the accountability board at his word; records that should exist simply don't. His menace is bureaucratic, patient, and total. Fendt was his instrument; the 'filed no accompanying record' was his method.

presencePattern.rarity: mid-arc — the power the live Fendt scandal points UP toward; beaten by exposure, not force

figures.voiceHints: Warm, administrative, faintly apologetic while he ruins you. Speaks of order and the greater good of the district.
knowledge: Every record in the Edge District and which ones must not exist
Who owes what and to whom
Exactly how much truth a place can bury before it notices
figures.wants: A quiet, orderly district where the right names are on the board and the wrong ones vanish.
figures.fears: A keeper of thirty years who remembers — an incorruptible witness the ledger cannot erase.
figures.threading: DIRECTLY threads the live Fendt event — Fendt is his instrument, the accountability-board anomaly is his fingerprint, and the thirty-year keeper who 'has never seen this before' is the thread that could bring him down. The player's current world is ALREADY seeded with this regional villain.
